[Insights](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/pulse) # Sidebar blur in linux \#12886 [GarrettGR](https://github.com/GarrettGR) started this conversation in [Ideas](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/categories/ideas) [Sidebar blur in linux](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/12886#top)#12886 [![@GarrettGR](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/46307948?s=40&v=4)\\ GarrettGR](https://github.com/GarrettGR) on Mar 22Mar 22, 2026· 2 comments [Return to top](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/12886#top) Discussion options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply ## [![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/46307948?s=64&v=4)\ GarrettGR](https://github.com/GarrettGR) [on Mar 22Mar 22, 2026](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/12886\#discussion-9723145) | | | --- | | I'm not sure if I've missed something, but I've looked through current issues, discussions, and PRs, read through the documentation, and searched for ways to do this -- and so far I haven't found anything.
I am currently using Zen everyday, and I have for over a year (and Arc before that) and I absolutely love it.
Really one of the only things that I've had issues with is the transparency on linux. If there was either a way to make it less transparent _or_ a way to add more blur to the transparency of the sidebar, that would be great.
* * *
I am using zen-twilight on NixOS (relevant config below):
![image](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/46307948/567457610-f57c49dc-96a0-483d-bf0f-bdbe4258d381.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..QkirdW2-7Ou-ygj9qVLltMsszmCNMJDJ2aD11PLODYM)
* * *
And, as you can see, I have `zen.widget.linux.transparency` configured (set to `true`):
![image](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/46307948/567457262-c5c5f5cd-b8b9-4fd8-a6fd-5f4470f62f01.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..WFQVMyAu85Z4B8vVCC-SFsT1OZmf7vSH5kr8rJIsdR8)
* * *
On my system, this looks like this:
![image](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/46307948/567457877-bfbe212f-342c-4f27-b189-d851baa24060.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..0XwcDgKSyP6WGtGdLr8XFTTJbguFO0cqZAbnQ0HbpcA)
* * *
As you can see there is some blur, and it is _mostly_ legible, but I do often struggle nonetheless. Here it is with my ghostty terminal next to it for comparison.
![image](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/46307948/567458188-ef15c6e5-3665-4acf-8a01-16167de022f5.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..jwP8b0n7ryGEidIVfAqkP78pAHr868RIllpt31AAZRs)
I have also tried configuring zen with dark colors (I have tried the same color as ghostty, although I don't have a picture of that unfortunately) and while it does have some improvement (more contrast with the text), it doesn't fully solve the issue.
* * *
TLDR; This is a small thing, but it really would go a long way for me in terms of usability of the browser, and I am sure other people as well. Ghostty and many other programs allow users to set a value between 0 and 1 for the opacity. Alternatively, some programs allow the user to configure the number, or type, of blurring passes on the transparent regions. | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/12886#) 2You must be logged in to vote All reactions ## Replies: 2 comments - [Oldest](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/12886?sort=old) - [Newest](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/12886?sort=new) - [Top](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/12886?sort=top) Comment options - Copy link - Copy Markdown # {{title}} Quote reply ### [![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/46307948?s=64&v=4)\ GarrettGR](https://github.com/GarrettGR) [on Mar 22Mar 22, 2026](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/12886\#discussioncomment-16263332) Author | | | --- | | Oh also related discussions are: [#3397](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/3397) (closed), [#8108](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/8108), and [#8734](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/8734) (related so-far as its regarding configuring transparency). | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/12886#) 1You must be logged in to vote All reactions 0 replies Comment options # {{title}} Quote reply ### [![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/51459108?s=64&v=4)\ kuqquk](https://github.com/kuqquk) [last weekJun 18, 2026](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/12886\#discussioncomment-17356054) - | | | --- | | On KDE Plasma, enabling zen.widget.linux.transparency in Zen and adding Zen to the "force blur" option using " [https://github.com/xarblu/kwin-effects-better-blur-dx](https://github.com/xarblu/kwin-effects-better-blur-dx)" blurs the Zen tab bar. | BetaWas this translation helpful?
